rulings.Case Title: FRANK STONICH et al VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 1. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.,'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Hearing required to address whether sustaining the demurrer should be with or without leave to amend.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JUANITA ROBERTSON et al VS. GREEN TREE SERVICING, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And To Dismiss The Complaint For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT GREEN TREE SERVICING' LLC's Motion To Strike Or Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And To Dismiss The Complaint For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court Granted. Once a general demurrer is sustained with leave to amend and Plaintiffs do not so amend within the time authorized by the court or otherwise extended by stipulation or appropriate order, they can no longer voluntarily dismiss their action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 581, subdivision 1, even if the trial court has yet to enter a judgment of dismissal on the sustained demurrer." (Wells v. Marina City Properties, Inc. (1981) 29 Cal.3d 781, 789.) The voluntary dismissal on August 1, 2013 was accepted in error and is vacated. The Complaint is dismissed for failure to amend.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PETE DEMARTINI et al VS. KELLY MCCABE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For An Order To Enter Judgment For Restitution Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 4. PLAINTIFFs PETE DEMARTINI and PAUL DEMARTINI's Motion For An Order To Enter Judgment For Restitution Pursuant To Settlement Agreement Motion for an Order to Enter Judgment for Restitution Pursuant to Settlement Agreement Continued on the Court's own motion to November 12, 2013 to provide opposing parties an opportunity to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B and provide Department 501 with a courtesy copy of their opposition. The courtesy copy is due to Department 501 no later than Tuesday, November 5, 2013 at 4:00pm. Opposing party must provide a cover letter stating the new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY LEONG LOUIE VS. RAYMOND HONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Ntc Of Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 12. DEFENDANTs RAYMOND HONG, HILDEGARD HONG and DOMINIC HONG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the documents provided. The contents of these documents are not the proper subject of judicial notice. Even if the Court took judicial notice, Defendants failed produce evidence that shows the building Defendants reside in is subject to the rent ordinance. There is no indication that the building referenced in the documents is the buildings in which Defendants reside. There are two structures that share the address 570 38th Street. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SPINDLER PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. ALISON C WRIGHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 5. rulings.Case Title: WALTER E. HUGHES JR. VS. RADOUANE SADRANE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 7. DEFENDANTs RADOUANE SADRANE and INGA LARSEN's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Granted, subject to opposition. Plaintiff cannot evict Defendants pursuant to San Francisco Rent Ordinance section 37.9(a)(8) because Plaintiffs are protected under San Francisco Rent Ordinance section 37.9 (i) and (j).= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KLL MONTGOMERY ,LLC VS. MARK COLLINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions Agfainst Deft
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 04, 2013, line 8. PLAINTIFF KLL MONTGOMERY ,LLC's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Granted, in part, subject to opposition. Plaintiff is ordered to respond to Defendant's Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) and Request for Admissions (Set One) by noon on November 8, 2013. Matters addressed in the Request for Admissions are not deemed admitted. Sanctions in the amount of $885 are granted.= (501/REQ)
